The phonetic realization of 'c' in 'riruuhci' as '[ci]' is inconsistent with typical Parks notation where 'c' usually represents '[ts]' or '[č]'. The previous entry for the same headword 'riruuhci' (SK-riruuhci-p494-4204) shows '[čɪ]'.
Noun headword 'riwaaciiriku' ends in a bare vowel 'u' instead of the expected glottal stop 'ʔ'.
Headword 'taakaar' has a long vowel 'aa' in the first syllable, but the phonetic form '[•ta-kaa(r)•]' shows a short vowel 'a'.
Headword 'taakaruucaas' has 'c' followed by 'aa', but the phonetic form '[taa-kà-ruu-čaas]' shows 'čaa'. 'c' before 'a' is typically realized as 'ts', not 'č', in Skiri Pawnee.
